problem_text_en: 4. In the following four nuclear reaction equations, $X_1, X_2, X_3, X_4$ represent the corresponding particles: $_{1}^{2}\text{H} + _{1}^{3}\text{H} \to _{2}^{4}\text{He} + X_1$; $_{7}^{14}\text{N} + _{2}^{4}\text{He} \to _{8}^{17}\text{O} + X_2$; $_{13}^{27}\text{Al} + _{2}^{4}\text{He} \to _{15}^{30}\text{P} + X_3$; $_{92}^{235}\text{U} + X_4 \to _{38}^{90}\text{Sr} + _{54}^{136}\text{Xe} + 10_{0}^{1}\text{n}$. The correct judgment is (  ) A. $X_1, X_2, X_3$ are all neutrons; B. $X_2, X_3, X_4$ are all neutrons; C. $X_3, X_4, X_1$ are all neutrons; D. $X_4, X_1, X_2$ are all neutrons.
problem_solution_en: [Analysis and Solution] This problem examines the conservation laws (conservation of charge number, conservation of mass number) in nuclear reaction equations. 1. Find $X_1$: Mass number $2+3-4=1$, charge number $1+1-2=0$, so $X_1$ is a neutron ($_{0}^{1}\text{n}$). 2. Find $X_2$: Mass number $14+4-17=1$, charge number $7+2-8=1$, so $X_2$ is a proton ($_{1}^{1}\text{H}$). 3. Find $X_3$: Mass number $27+4-30=1$, charge number $13+2-15=0$, so $X_3$ is a neutron ($_{0}^{1}\text{n}$). 4. Find $X_4$: Mass number $90+136+10-235=1$, charge number $38+54-92=0$, so $X_4$ is a neutron ($_{0}^{1}\text{n}$). In summary, $X_1, X_3, X_4$ are all neutrons, corresponding to option C.
